session.user:
Perform the following systematic renames across the entire codebase using LibCST:
1. Rename class `PlanBackend` to `ManagedPrBackend` (including all imports, type annotations, and references) 2. Rename class `PlannedPRBackend` to `GitHubManagedPrBackend` (including all imports, type annotations, and references)
These classes are defined in: - `PlanBackend` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py` - `PlannedPRBackend` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr.py`
The rename should cover: - Class definitions - All imports of these classes - Type annotations that reference them - isinstance checks - docstrings and comments that reference the class names - String literals that contain the class names
Search scope: `src/`, `packages/`, `tests/`
Important: Do NOT rename: - Method names (those will be done separately) - The `get_provider_name()` return value `"github-draft-pr"` (serialization name, must be preserved) - The `plan_store` package/directory name (out of scope)
session.assistant: I'll help you perform these systematic renames using LibCST. Let me start by exploring the codebase structure and understanding the scope of changes needed.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Abstract interface for plan storage backends. 2→ 3→PlanBackend is a BACKEND, not a gateway. Backends compose gateways (like GitHubIssues) 4→and should NOT have fake implementations. To test code that uses a PlanBackend, 5→inject fake gateways into the real backend implementation. 6→ 7→Example: 8→ # Testing with fake gateway 9→ fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues() 10→ fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub(issues_gateway=fake_issues) 11→ backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) 12→ result = backend.create_plan(...) 13→ 14→ # Assert on gateway mutations 15→ assert fake_issues.created_issues[0][0] == "expected title" 16→ 17→See: .claude/skills/fake-driven-testing/references/gateway-architecture.md 18→for the full gateway vs backend architecture. 19→""" 20→ 21→from __future__ import annotations 22→ 23→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 24→from collections.abc import Mapping 25→from pathlib import Path 26→ 27→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import ( 28→ CREATED_FROM_SESSION, 29→ LAST_LEARN_SESSION, 30→ LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_SESSION,
... [442 lines omitted] ...
99→ RuntimeError: If provider fails 165→ RuntimeError: If provider fails or plan not found 196→ RuntimeError: If plan not found 201→ raise RuntimeError(msg) 310→ RuntimeError: If provider fails 329→ PlanHeaderNotFoundError: If plan has no plan-header metadata block 330→ RuntimeError: If provider fails or plan not found 352→ RuntimeError: If plan not found or update fails 365→ RuntimeError: If provider fails, plan not found, or invalid identifier 384→ RuntimeError: If plan not found or update fails 406→ RuntimeError: If provider fails or plan not found 425→ RuntimeError: If provider fails or plan not found 442→ RuntimeError: If plan not found 464→ RuntimeError: If provider fails or plan not found

1→"""Draft PR implementation of plan storage. 2→ 3→Uses GitHub draft pull requests as the backing store for plans. 4→PR body contains the plan-header metadata block followed by plan content. 5→The "[erk-pr]" title prefix identifies plan PRs vs regular draft PRs. 6→""" 7→ 8→import logging 9→from collections.abc import Mapping 10→from datetime import UTC 11→from pathlib import Path 12→ 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.label_ops import add_labels_resilient 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 17→ add_metadata_block, 18→ find_metadata_block, 19→ has_metadata_block, 20→ render_metadata_block, 21→ replace_metadata_block_in_body, 22→) 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import ( 24→ format_plan_header_body, 25→) 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import ( 27→ CREATED_FROM_SESSION, 28→ CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL, 29→ LEARNED_FROM_ISSUE, 30→ OBJECTIVE_ISSUE,

It has no 12→fake implementation. Test by injecting fake gateways into real backends. 13→""" 14→

session.assistant: I see these are comments in test files. The script successfully transformed all code but didn't handle comments. LibCST doesn't directly transform comments - they're part of the whitespace/trivia. Let me update the files with comments manually using a simple text replacement since those are just documentation:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: find src/ packages/ tests/ -name "*.py" -type f -exec grep -l "PlannedPRBackend" {} \;

session.tool_result:
diff --git a/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py b/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py index ea251fe70..0a674d4de 100644 --- a/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py +++ b/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py @@ -1,14 +1,14 @@ """Abstract interface for plan storage backends.
-PlanBackend is a BACKEND, not a gateway. Backends compose gateways (like GitHubIssues) -and should NOT have fake implementations. To test code that uses a PlanBackend, +ManagedPrBackend is a BACKEND, not a gateway. Backends compose gateways (like GitHubIssues) +and should NOT have fake implementations. To test code that uses a ManagedPrBackend, inject fake gateways into the real backend implementation.
Example: # Testing with fake gateway fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues() fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub(issues_gateway=fake_issues) - backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) + backend = GitHubManagedPrBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = backend.create_plan(...)
# Assert on gateway mutations @@ -48,7 +48,7 @@ from erk_shared.sessions.discovery import SessionsForPlan # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-class PlanBackend(ABC): +class ManagedPrBackend(ABC): """Abstract interface for plan storage operations.
... [9 lines omitted] ...
